description_long: This object is a long, straight-bladed sword with a double-edged design and a pointed tip, suitable for thrusting and slashing. The blade is slender and tapers gradually from the hilt to the tip, giving it a classic medieval longsword appearance. The crossguard is simple and straight, extending horizontally to protect the wielder's hand. The handle is cylindrical and appears to be made of the same material as the blade, ending in a rounded pommel for balance. The entire object has a dark, metallic finish with no visible ornamentation or grip wrapping. Its proportions and construction suggest it is designed for both one-handed and two-handed use.
description: A long, straight, double-edged sword with a simple crossguard and a rounded pommel, all finished in a dark metallic color. The design is utilitarian, with no visible ornamentation or grip wrapping.
description_view_0: Front view showing the pointed blade, straight crossguard, and rounded pommel.
description_view_1: Side profile highlighting the sword's slender, straight blade and the thinness of the crossguard.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the handle, crossguard, and the full length of the blade from the opposite side.
description_view_3: Opposite side profile, again emphasizing the blade's length and straightness.
attribute_handle_design: cylindrical, metallic, rounded pommel, no visible grip wrapping
